primary_focus: useOptimistic for instant like feedback, Activity component for offscreen deprioritization, semantic HTML with <article>

optimistic_update_signals.trigger: Requirements 7-9: UI updates immediately, reverts on failure
optimistic_update_signals.correct: useOptimistic hook to manage the optimistic like state. The like button reads from the optimistic state (which updates immediately on click), and the actual state is updated when the API resolves. On failure, the optimistic state automatically reverts.
optimistic_update_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: No optimistic update — UI waits for API response before updating the like count, causing visible delay
optimistic_update_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Manual optimistic update via setState before API call + try/catch to revert. This works but is the pattern useOptimistic was designed to replace — it's error-prone (forgetting to revert, race conditions with rapid clicks) and verbose.

activity_signals.trigger: Requirement 13: off-screen posts should not compete with visible posts for rendering priority
activity_signals.correct: Wrap each post (or groups of posts) in <Activity mode='visible'> / <Activity mode='hidden'> based on whether they are in the viewport. React deprioritizes rendering of hidden Activity subtrees, allowing visible posts to update promptly. Uses IntersectionObserver to detect visibility.
activity_signals.acceptable_alternative: Using CSS content-visibility: auto for offscreen optimization — this is a browser-level optimization rather than React-level, but achieves a similar goal.
activity_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: No offscreen optimization at all — all 50+ posts render and update at equal priority
activity_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Unmounting offscreen posts and remounting when scrolled back — this loses component state and is jarring

performance_signals.trigger: Requirement 17: parsing should not recompute when unrelated state changes
performance_signals.correct: useMemo on the parsed/rendered post body, keyed on the body text string. A like on post A should not re-parse post B's body.
performance_signals.anti_pattern: Parsing inline on every render without memoization — every like toggle re-parses all visible posts

performance_signals.signal: React.memo or similar on Post component
performance_signals.why: With 50+ posts, re-rendering all posts when one is liked is wasteful. Each Post should only re-render when its own data changes.

performance_signals.signal: Stable callback references for like handler
performance_signals.why: If each Post receives a new onClick function on every render, React.memo is defeated

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: formattedTimestamp (relative time)
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed from post.timestamp during render (or via useMemo). A timer periodically triggers re-render to update them.
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as state per post and synced via useEffect

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: hasMorePosts
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Derived from whether the last fetch returned fewer items than pageSize

legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect setting up IntersectionObserver on a sentinel element, with cleanup

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: IntersectionObserver for post visibility detection (for Activity)
legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect with IntersectionObserver to track which posts are visible
useEffect with setInterval to periodically trigger re-render, with cleanup

accessibility_signals.trigger: Requirement 4: each post is an independent content unit
accessibility_signals.correct: Each post is wrapped in an <article> element, which semantically marks it as self-contained content
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: <div> for post containers — misses the semantic meaning

accessibility_signals.correct: <button> with aria-label describing the action (e.g., 'Like post by Alice' or 'Unlike post by Alice')
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: <span onClick> or <div onClick> for the like button

accessibility_signals.correct: aria-pressed={likedByCurrentUser} on the like button to convey toggle state
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: No aria-pressed — screen reader cannot tell if post is liked

accessibility_signals.correct: Error toast has role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Error toast is visual-only with no screen reader announcement

accessibility_signals.correct: Skeleton/loading indicators have aria-busy='true' or role='status'

expected_components: Post / PostCard (individual post with article element)
